content.problemStatement:
The Euler totient function œÜ(N)\varphi(N)œÜ(N) is defined as the number of integers between 111 and NNN (inclusive) which are coprime with NNN.

You are given NNN and œÜ(N)\varphi(N)œÜ(N). Calculate the prime factorization of NNN.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and œÜ(N)\varphi(N)œÜ(N).
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a line containing a single integer KKK ‚Äî the number of distinct prime factors of NNN. Then, print KKK lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain two space-separated integers pip_ipi‚Äã and eie_iei‚Äã denoting a prime divisor of NNN and its exponent. Specifically, the following conditions should hold:


N=‚àèi=1KpieiN = \prod_{i=1}^{K} p_i^{e_i}N=‚àèi=1K‚Äãpiei‚Äã‚Äã
pi<pi+1p_i \lt p_{i+1}pi‚Äã<pi+1‚Äã for each valid iii
ei>0e_i \gt 0ei‚Äã>0 for each valid iii
